summary.intent: Get PR #724 merged by fixing CI failures and passing Codex review on the fogodev/ars-ui repository.
summary.outcome: Fixed the Adapter Parity CI failure by adding a Dioxus Field wasm test mirroring an existing Leptos test; all local gates (format, clippy, parity) passed and the fix was committed and pushed.

repo: The repo has an 'adapter-parity' xtask gate that enforces the test count delta between Leptos and Dioxus adapters stays within a threshold — adding Leptos tests without matching Dioxus tests will break CI.
Dioxus renders boolean HTML attributes as `hidden="true"` rather than the empty-string encoding Leptos uses; assertions must use a framework-appropriate boolean-attribute helper.
The repo uses a pre-commit formatting hook that must complete before pushing — bypassing it with --no-verify is not acceptable practice here.
Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` PR comment and signals active review with an 'eyes' reaction on the issue.

code.finding: Dioxus Field wasm tests use button-driven signal updates to keep behaviors browser-observable; new parity tests must follow this style.
Dioxus Field component API uses a `value:` prop pattern; reviewed to understand how to wire a reactive error-visibility test.

workflow: After a formatting commit changes the branch head, any in-flight Codex review must be re-triggered since it was started against the previous commit.
CI failures should be addressed immediately rather than waiting for cascading checks to complete — pulling the failing job log early avoids wasted polling time.
Running the focused wasm test (`--test test_field_wasm`) before the broader parity gate gives faster feedback on new test correctness.

friction: Formatting commit was required mid-session, which changed the branch head and invalidated the already-triggered Codex review, requiring a second trigger.
Dioxus boolean attribute rendering difference (`hidden="true"` vs empty string) caused the new parity test to fail on first run, requiring an assertion fix.
Polling CI/Codex required manual sleep intervals with no event-driven notification, making the wait loop tedious.
open_items: Codex review result not yet confirmed — the session ended while Codex still had the 'eyes' reaction, meaning the review outcome and any new threads are unresolved.
Clippy was still pending in CI at the time the adapter parity failure was discovered — its final result after the parity fix was not confirmed.
The underlying reason Field accumulated more Leptos tests than Dioxus tests (the review-driven behaviors) suggests a broader parity gap that was only partially addressed by adding one test.
